top_signals.title: Goldman Offers Preferred Stock Days After Post-Crisis Spread Low
top_signals.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is looking to sell new preferred shares just days after a metric determining risk and value in these securities hit its lowest level since the global financial crisis.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels threaten blockade against Saudi Arabia
top_signals.summary: Renewed fighting puts kingdom’s crude exports through Red Sea at risk
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Zelenskyy’s crisis deepens as ex-defence minister rejects offer to return
top_signals.summary: Ukrainian president under pressure to replace commander-in-chief who clashed with Mykhailo Fedorov
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack AI system to rival Nvidia, adding Microsoft as newest buyer
top_signals.summary: AMD is taking aim at Nvidia with Helios, its first AI rack system, and has landed Microsoft as a new customer, joining Meta, OpenAI and Oracle.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Why Mullin is struggling to keep ICE out of the headlines
top_signals.summary: Two killings by federal agents in the space of a week have drawn attention to the downsides of a DHS hiring spree
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Shapoorji Closes One of India’s Biggest Private Credit Deals
top_signals.summary: India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group on Monday completed one of the country’s largest private credit transactions, raising around 151 billion rupees ($1.6 billion), according to people familiar with the matter, in a sign that domestic investors remain willing to fund large leveraged deals despite volatile global markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How I got into Berghain, Berlin’s notoriously selective club

top_signals.title: Hugging Face says an autonomous AI agent hacked its network
top_signals.summary: The Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository disclosed that attackers gained access to internal datasets and credentials after breaching its production infrastructure using an autonomous AI agent system. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Netflix and Gary Lineker’s Goalhanger extend The Rest Is Football deal until 2028
top_signals.summary: Gary Lineker’s The Rest Is Football podcast will continue on Netflix for the next two seasons following the success of its run during the World Cup. The hit show, which also stars former England internationals Alan Shearer and Micah Richards, will focus on the Premier League and Champions League in twice-weekly episodes on the streaming [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: New York imports more electricity from Canada after high-voltage transmission line opens
top_signals.summary: On July 3, 2026, the New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) imported 52 gigawatthours (GWh) of electricity from Canada, the most traded between the two areas since January 2025. Some of the imported electricity flowed along the new Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE) transmission line between Quebec, Canada, and New York City, which officially reached commercial operations in May after three years of construction.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

alerts.title: Baltic Dry Index falls to 2671 down 81 points
alerts.summary: Today, Monday, July 20 2026, the Baltic Dry Index decreased by 81 points, reaching 2671 points. Baltic Dry Index is compiled by the London-based Baltic Exchange and covers prices for transported cargo such as coal, grain and iron ore. The index is based on a daily survey of agents all over the world. Baltic Dry ...
